title: galactia marginalis subsp. longipedunculata
intro: Galactia marginalis subsp. longipedunculata, also known as the long-stalked milkpea, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Galactia marginalis subsp. longipedunculata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
